(born 20 August 1948)[[1]](#cite_note-star-1) is an Australian actor. He is best known for his roles as [Denethor](/wiki/Denethor "Denethor") in [*The Lord of the Rings* film trilogy](/wiki/The_Lord_of_the_Rings_(film_series) "The Lord of the Rings (film series)") (2001–2003), and [Dr. Walter Bishop](/wiki/Walter_Bishop_(Fringe) "Walter Bishop (Fringe)") in the [Fox](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company") science fiction series *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")* (2008–2013). His other television credits include the supernatural drama *[Sleepy Hollow](/wiki/Sleepy_Hollow_(TV_series) "Sleepy Hollow (TV series)")* (2013–2017) and the police procedural *[Elementary](/wiki/Elementary_(TV_series) "Elementary (TV series)")* (2015–2019). Noble has also lent his distinctively deep voice to animated and video game projects, most notably as Leland Monroe in [Rockstar Games](/wiki/Rockstar_Games "Rockstar Games")' *[L.A. Noire](/wiki/L.A._Noire "L.A. Noire")* (2011), [Unicron](/wiki/Unicron "Unicron") in the animated series *[Transformers: Prime](/wiki/Transformers:_Prime "Transformers: Prime")* (2010–2013), and [Scarecrow](/wiki/Scarecrow_(DC_Comics) "Scarecrow (DC Comics)") in the [DC Comics](/wiki/DC_Comics "DC Comics") game *[Batman: Arkham Knight](/wiki/Batman:_Arkham_Knight "Batman: Arkham Knight")* (2015).

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Australian actor (born 1948)

For other uses, see [John Noble (disambiguation)](/wiki/John_Noble_(disambiguation) "John Noble (disambiguation)").

| John Noble | |
| --- | --- |
| Noble in 2012 | |
| Born | (1948-08-20) 20 August 1948 (age 77)  [Port Pirie](/wiki/Port_Pirie "Port Pirie"), South Australia, Australia[[1]](#cite_note-star-1)[*[unreliable source?](/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources "Wikipedia:Reliable sources")*] |
| Occupations | Actor, voice actor, theatre director |
| Years active | 1988–present |
| Spouse | Penny Noble |
| Children | 3, including [Samantha Noble](/wiki/Samantha_Noble "Samantha Noble") |

**John Noble** (born 20 August 1948)[[1]](#cite_note-star-1) is an Australian actor. He is best known for his roles as [Denethor](/wiki/Denethor "Denethor") in [*The Lord of the Rings* film trilogy](/wiki/The_Lord_of_the_Rings_(film_series) "The Lord of the Rings (film series)") (2001–2003), and [Dr. Walter Bishop](/wiki/Walter_Bishop_(Fringe) "Walter Bishop (Fringe)") in the [Fox](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company") science fiction series *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")* (2008–2013). His other television credits include the supernatural drama *[Sleepy Hollow](/wiki/Sleepy_Hollow_(TV_series) "Sleepy Hollow (TV series)")* (2013–2017) and the police procedural *[Elementary](/wiki/Elementary_(TV_series) "Elementary (TV series)")* (2015–2019). Noble has also lent his distinctively deep voice to animated and video game projects, most notably as Leland Monroe in [Rockstar Games](/wiki/Rockstar_Games "Rockstar Games")' *[L.A. Noire](/wiki/L.A._Noire "L.A. Noire")* (2011), [Unicron](/wiki/Unicron "Unicron") in the animated series *[Transformers: Prime](/wiki/Transformers:_Prime "Transformers: Prime")* (2010–2013), and [Scarecrow](/wiki/Scarecrow_(DC_Comics) "Scarecrow (DC Comics)") in the [DC Comics](/wiki/DC_Comics "DC Comics") game *[Batman: Arkham Knight](/wiki/Batman:_Arkham_Knight "Batman: Arkham Knight")* (2015).

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Noble&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Noble's early acting career started in theater throughout the 1970s and 1980s. For 10 years, he was an artistic director for the Stage Company of South Australia. Noble was a Trustee of the [Adelaide Festival Centre](/wiki/Adelaide_Festival_Centre "Adelaide Festival Centre") and chairman of the [Adelaide Festival of the Arts](/wiki/Adelaide_Festival "Adelaide Festival"). In 1979, he starred in *Errol Flynn's Great Big Adventure Book for Boys* at the [Edinburgh Festival](/wiki/Edinburgh_Festival "Edinburgh Festival") in Scotland. In 1984, Noble received a nomination by South Australian Premier [John Bannon](/wiki/John_Bannon "John Bannon"), for the [Young Australian of the Year](/wiki/List_of_Young_Australian_of_the_Year_Award_recipients "List of Young Australian of the Year Award recipients") award.[[1]](#cite_note-star-1)

Noble at age 40 made his film debut in the 1988 horror film *[The Dreaming](/wiki/The_Dreaming_(film) "The Dreaming (film)")*.[[1]](#cite_note-star-1)

He made occasional appearances on the television series *[All Saints](/wiki/All_Saints_(TV_series) "All Saints (TV series)")*. His performance as [Denethor](/wiki/Denethor "Denethor"), son of Ecthelion, in [*The Lord of the Rings* trilogy](/wiki/The_Lord_of_the_Rings_(film_series) "The Lord of the Rings (film series)") resulted in his becoming better known to international audiences. He played Russian Consul Anatoly Markov in the sixth season of the US television series *[24](/wiki/24_(TV_series) "24 (TV series)")*.

He won awards when starring as eccentric scientist Walter Bishop in the television series *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")* for 5 seasons (2008–2013).[[1]](#cite_note-star-1)
Noble played Morland Holmes, father of [Sherlock Holmes](/wiki/Sherlock_Holmes "Sherlock Holmes"), in *[Elementary](/wiki/Elementary_(TV_series) "Elementary (TV series)")*. He was a series regular for season 4 with guest appearances in seasons 6 and 7.[[2]](#cite_note-var-2)

Noble has also done [voice acting](/wiki/Voice_acting "Voice acting"), usually portraying villains. He voiced the Dragon Spirit in [M. Night Shyamalan](/wiki/M._Night_Shyamalan "M. Night Shyamalan")'s film *[The Last Airbender](/wiki/The_Last_Airbender_(film) "The Last Airbender (film)")* (2010), appeared as Real Estate tycoon Leland Monroe in [Rockstar Games](/wiki/Rockstar_Games "Rockstar Games")' *[L.A. Noire](/wiki/L.A._Noire "L.A. Noire")* (2011),[[3]](#cite_note-3) and provided the voice for [Unicron](/wiki/Unicron "Unicron") in the animated series *[Transformers: Prime](/wiki/Transformers:_Prime "Transformers: Prime")* and its conclusion film *[Transformers Prime Beast Hunters: Predacons Rising](/wiki/Transformers_Prime_Beast_Hunters:_Predacons_Rising "Transformers Prime Beast Hunters: Predacons Rising")*. Noble also voiced the Diviner in the 2021 animated series  *[Star Trek: Prodigy](/wiki/Star_Trek:_Prodigy "Star Trek: Prodigy")*. In 2024, he played an alternate version of the Diviner called Ilthuran.

Noble has done voice-over work for numerous [DC Comics](/wiki/DC_Comics "DC Comics") projects, including the animated film *[Superman: Unbound](/wiki/Superman:_Unbound "Superman: Unbound")* (2013) as the [supervillain](/wiki/Supervillain "Supervillain") [Brainiac](/wiki/Brainiac_(character) "Brainiac (character)"), the video game *[Batman: Arkham Knight](/wiki/Batman:_Arkham_Knight "Batman: Arkham Knight")* (2015) as the primary antagonist [Scarecrow](/wiki/Scarecrow_(DC_Comics) "Scarecrow (DC Comics)"),[[4]](#cite_note-VAVG247-4) and the television series *[Legends of Tomorrow](/wiki/Legends_of_Tomorrow "Legends of Tomorrow")* as the demon [Mallus](/wiki/Mallus_(Arrowverse) "Mallus (Arrowverse)"). Additionally, Noble appeared as himself in the *Legends of Tomorrow* episode "Guest Starring John Noble".

On 21 February 2025 Noble made his debut on season two of the [Apple TV+](/wiki/Apple_TV%2B "Apple TV+") show *[Severance](/wiki/Severance_(TV_series) "Severance (TV series)")* in the sixth episode, titled "[Attila](/wiki/Attila_(Severance) "Attila (Severance)")", playing the role of a character named Cecil Fields.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Fields is a [homosexual](/wiki/Homosexual "Homosexual") man who is married to [Christopher Walken](/wiki/Christopher_Walken "Christopher Walken")'s character, Burt Goodman, who is often identified as "Burt G."[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Noble&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Noble divides his time between residences in [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") and [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney"), Australia, with wife Penny Noble. They have three children, including actress [Samantha Noble](/wiki/Samantha_Noble "Samantha Noble"). In 2011, Noble's hobbies were reported to be "music, painting, and narration".[[9]](#cite_note-9) He studies [theoretical physics](/wiki/Theoretical_physics "Theoretical physics") and requested that the writers of *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")* always keep things grounded in what could be scientifically feasible.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

In 2012, Noble was diagnosed with [osteoporosis](/wiki/Osteoporosis "Osteoporosis"). His charity, Noble Bones, helps to raise awareness of the disease.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Noble&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Filmography")]

### Film

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Noble&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Film")]

| Year | Title | Role | Notes |
